meta.description: Mock WeAt dev-team conversation for integration testing. Covers a P1 Redis incident, fix, deploy, and sprint planning across one week.
meta.notes: Timestamps are illustrative. The seed_chat.py fixture sends messages in order with real server timestamps spaced ~200ms apart.

messages.body: morning team, anyone else seeing elevated API latency? p99 is at 2.3s, normally under 200ms
yeah I got paged at 3am. redis connection pool is exhausted — pool_size is set to 10 but we're spawning 40 workers in prod
frontend is timing out on /api/search too, users are complaining in #support
this is a P1. @weat_dan can you bump the redis pool size in infra config without a deploy? and @weat_bob start on a proper fix
found the root cause — the background job scheduler is creating a new RedisClient per job instead of sharing the singleton. will fix
on it, bumping pool_size to 50 in prod config now
done. pool_size=50 in prod. latency dropping
p99 back to 185ms. working on the permanent fix — moving RedisClient init to module level so all workers share one pool
frontend timeouts are clearing up. nice work everyone
good. let's do a full postmortem once the fix is in. @weat_bob ping me when the PR is ready
PR #247 is up. core change is in services/cache.py — singleton RedisClient, shared across all job workers. also added pool utilization metrics so we can alert before it hits 100%
reviewing now
looks solid. one thing: add a connection health check at worker startup — if redis is down, fail fast instead of hanging. left inline comments
good catch, added health check. also included a circuit breaker so repeated failures don't hammer a down redis
approved. @weat_dan can you schedule a deploy for tonight, low traffic window?
yep, 11pm PST. I'll monitor the pool metrics during rollout
deploy done, v2.4.1 is live. redis pool utilization sitting at 11%, all healthy. zero errors during rollout
excellent. @weat_bob can you write up the postmortem? add it to the wiki under Incidents/2026-05-26-redis-pool
on it, will have it done by EOD
should we add a status page entry? some users were confused about the outage window yesterday
good idea. @weat_dan can you draft one?
already drafted, will publish once you approve @weat_alice. sending link in DM
sprint planning in 1hr everyone. please review the backlog first — top items are the search revamp and mobile perf
quick q — are we still targeting the search revamp this sprint? product has been asking every day
yes, it's the top priority. @weat_bob you're leading that, right?
yep. scoped it out this week — 3 sprints total. first sprint is the indexing pipeline refactor, then ranking, then UI. I can walk through the design doc in the meeting
what about the mobile perf work? we've been kicking that can for two sprints now
mobile perf is lower priority than search but if we have capacity let's pull in the quick wins. @weat_dan do you have bandwidth?
yes, my plate is lighter after the infra cleanup. I can take the asset compression and CDN cache tuning items, those are well-scoped
perfect. let's finalize in the meeting. see everyone in 45min
